title: Epitaxial $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ with intrinsic magnetocrystalline anisotropy as a route to bias-field-free nonlinear half-metal magnonics at the nanoscale
authors: Anna Maria Friedel, Jaafar Ghanbaja, Björn Heinz, Moritz Bechberger, Sylvie Migot, Sébastien Petit-Watelot, Stéphane Andrieu, Philipp Pirro
abstract: Half-metallic Heusler compounds like $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ allow to bridge magnonic and spintronic functionality for hybrid unconventional computing approaches with sought-after properties like 100% spin polarization and associated low Gilbert damping $α\leq 10^{-3}$. However, the desirable material parameters are inherently tied to the crystal lattice with a particularly critical dependence on structural order in $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$. To date, the successful fabrication of nanoscale devices with robust structural integrity remains yet a challenge, and consequently the impact of the material parameters on the resulting nonlinear spin-wave dynamics remains largely unexplored. Here, we report on a study of linear and nonlinear spin-wave dynamics in transversally magnetized $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ waveguides with impeccable crystalline ordering. We show that epitaxial, $\mathrm{L2}_1$-ordered $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ exhibits an intrinsic cubic anisotropy with first- and second-order contributions, stabilizing a magnetization alignment along the crystal $\langle110\rangle$ directions. We confirm the implication of an unaffected crystal structure resulting in preserved magnetic properties in the patterned structures. Herein, the persistent magnetocrystalline anisotropy reshapes the spin-wave dispersion which yields a first-order nonlinear instability suppression range extending over several GHz - even for vanishing bias fields. Moreover, the intrinsic magnetocrystalline anisotropy can be exploited to counteract shape demagnetization for a stabilized low bias field operation in the favourable Damon-Eshbach geometry with high group velocities and decay lengths. Together with the proven half-metallicity and ultralow Gilbert damping, this research establishes $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ as a robust, scalable platform towards bias-field-free nonlinear half-metal magnonics.
summary: This research demonstrates that epitaxial $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ retains its critical structural order even after nanoscale patterning, which is crucial for its magnetic functionality. The intrinsic cubic magnetocrystalline anisotropy is shown to stabilize spin-wave dynamics, enabling nonlinear magnonic operation at near-zero bias fields. This establishes $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ as a highly promising, scalable platform for next-generation spintronic devices.
main_problem: The central goal is to establish $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ as a robust, scalable platform for bias-field-free nonlinear half-metal magnonics at the nanoscale.
main_result: The intrinsic cubic magnetocrystalline anisotropy in epitaxial $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ stabilizes spin-wave dynamics, suppressing first-order instabilities and enabling low-bias field operation in the favorable Damon-Eshbach geometry.
method: The study combines epitaxial growth (MBE), top-down nanofabrication (EBL/Ar+ etching), and advanced characterization techniques including $\mu$BLS, FMR, and TEM to probe structure and dynamics.
model_or_system: The physical system is epitaxial $\mathrm{L2_1}$-ordered $\mathrm{Co_2MnSi}$ Heusler thin films patterned into nanoscale waveguides. The analysis relies on spin-wave dispersion models incorporating magnetocrystalline anisotropy and dipolar effects.
key_observables: Spin-wave dispersion, nonlinear instability onset (first and second order), spin-wave decay lengths ($\delta_{amp}$), and magnetization switching fields.
important_parameters: Intrinsic cubic magnetocrystalline anisotropy, ultralow Gilbert damping ($\alpha \le 10^{-3}$), and operation at vanishing external bias fields.
main_assumptions: The primary assumption is that the desirable magnetic properties, particularly the crystal structure, are preserved during the challenging top-down nanoscale patterning process.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the nanoscale integrity of the L21-ordered structure after patterning, quantify the cubic magnetocrystalline anisotropy via FMR, and show $\mu$BLS spectra confirming nonlinear instabilities and stabilized spin-wave propagation.
paper_structure: The paper systematically moves from confirming the structural integrity of the material after nanofabrication (TEM/STEM) to quantifying the intrinsic magnetic anisotropy (FMR/VSM), and finally demonstrating the functional consequences of this anisotropy on nonlinear spin-wave dynamics ($\mu$BLS/Kerr).
why_interesting: While focused on condensed matter magnetism, the study of nonlinear wave dynamics and the use of advanced spectroscopic techniques ($\mu$BLS) to probe collective excitations in patterned systems has parallels with studying nonlinear wave propagation in quantum optical cavities or superconducting circuits.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
